title: Advance page-number pagination for plays/guild --all (Greptile P1, re-review)
rationale: BGG's plays and guild commands paginate by page number with a server-fixed 100-item page and no page-size (limit) parameter, and the XML-normalized response exposes no next-cursor or has-more field. paginatedGet's only page-advance path was nextFullPageOffsetCursor, which hard-gates on paginationType == "offset" and a limit param, so it always returned false for these commands. The loop therefore broke after page 1 and `plays --all` / `guild --all` silently returned only the first 100 records.

changes.summary: Added nextFullPagePageCursor (page-number advance for page pagination with no limit param) and paginationTotalFromEnvelope/totalFromAttributes (extract @total from flat and XML-normalized nested envelopes). paginatedGet now tracks an inferred page size and, in both the array and object branches, advances via nextFullPagePageCursor as a fallback after nextFullPageOffsetCursor. When the envelope declares a total (BGG's @total) the walk stops exactly; otherwise a full-page heuristic is used. Restricted to paginationType=="page" with empty limitParam, so offset/limit and cursor/has-more pagination are untouched.
changes.validated_outcome: go build/vet/test pass; multi-page walk collects all 250 records across 3 pages and stops at the declared total without an extra fetch; single short page makes exactly one request.

changes.summary: New hand-authored tests: TestPaginatedGetPageTypeWalksAllPages (multi-page collect + exact stop), TestPaginatedGetPageTypeSinglePage (no speculative fetch), TestNextFullPagePageCursorGuards (gating matrix), TestPaginationTotalFromEnvelope (flat + nested @total extraction).
changes.validated_outcome: go test -count=1 ./internal/cli/ passes including the new pagination tests.
